title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Contract Workshop
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a protocol-driven circulation application while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity.
Define abstract base classes with required operations and optional shared behavior when nominal hierarchy is justified.
Define structural Protocol roles that match consumer needs and support static checking without forced inheritance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
